From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Italian composer

| Armando Fragna | |
| --- | --- |
| Born | (1898-08-02)2 August 1898  [Torre Annunziata](/wiki/Torre_Annunziata "Torre Annunziata"), [Naples](/wiki/Naples "Naples") |
| Died | 15 August 1972(1972-08-15) (aged 74)  [Livorno](/wiki/Livorno "Livorno") |

**Armando Fragna** (2 August 1898 – 15 August 1972) was an Italian composer, conductor and musician.

## Life and career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Armando_Fragna&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Life and career")]

Born in [Torre Annunziata](/wiki/Torre_Annunziata "Torre Annunziata"), [Naples](/wiki/Naples "Naples"), at just 18 years old Fragna started a long collaboration with [Ettore Petrolini](/wiki/Ettore_Petrolini "Ettore Petrolini") as the score composer of his theatrical works.[[1]](#cite_note-music-1)[[2]](#cite_note-Radio-2) In 1930 he started a career as a film score composer.[[1]](#cite_note-music-1)[[2]](#cite_note-Radio-2) In 1942 he entered [EIAR](/wiki/EIAR "EIAR") as the conductor of its "Orchestra Nostrana".[[2]](#cite_note-Radio-2) He also composed several pop songs, and two of them entered the competition at the [Sanremo Music Festival](/wiki/Sanremo_Music_Festival "Sanremo Music Festival").[[1]](#cite_note-music-1) Among his most successful songs, "I pompieri di Viggiù" and "I cadetti di Guascogna".[[1]](#cite_note-music-1)
